label: COVID mRNA Doses Administered
deltaNote: Demand declining sharply from 2022 peak as pandemic wanes

label: Countries with mRNA Vaccine Authorization
deltaNote: COVID mRNA vaccines authorized in virtually all major economies

label: Active Non-COVID mRNA Clinical Trials
deltaNote: Cancer vaccines, HIV, RSV, malaria, rare diseases driving pipeline growth

label: V940/mRNA-4157 Melanoma Death Rate Reduction (5-Year Follow-Up)
contestNote: Phase 2 data; Phase 3 INTerpath still enrolling. Initial 2022 readout showed 44% recurrence reduction; 5-yr follow-up shows 49% death rate reduction vs. pembro alone
deltaNote: Durable benefit confirmed at 5 years; Phase 3 primary results expected 2026–2027

label: Approved Non-COVID mRNA Vaccines
deltaNote: Mresvia (RSV, USA/EU) and Kostaive (saRNA COVID, Japan) approved 2023–2024

label: Days from Genome Sequence to First mRNA EUA
contestNote: SARS-CoV-2 genome published Jan 10, 2020; BNT162b2 EUA Dec 11, 2020

label: Companies with Active mRNA Therapeutic Pipeline
deltaNote: COVID success catalyzed massive investment in mRNA-based therapeutics

label: Peak Annual mRNA Vaccine Revenue (2022)
contestNote: Includes Moderna ($18.4B) + BioNTech/Pfizer Comirnaty ($37.8B) revenues; total market peak

label: NCI Cancer Vaccine Funding Commitment (2026)
contestNote: Partially reverses 2025 cuts: proposed 40% NCI budget cut, $590M BARDA pandemic flu contract cancellation, 22 mRNA contracts terminated
deltaNote: Covers personalized neoantigen vaccines, shared antigen approaches, and combination immunotherapy strategies
